proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_078 (a continuous demographic-like variable, range 30-90, plausibly age in years) is associated with longer pfs_months.
Mean pfs_months differs across the five categories of feature_018 (race: white, hispanic, black, asian, other).
Mean pfs_months differs across the four categories of feature_045 (insurance: medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured).

analyses.code:
from scipy import stats
stats.pearsonr(df['feature_078'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_078 is very strongly POSITIVELY correlated with pfs_months (Pearson r=+0.856, p<1e-300). Mean PFS rises monotonically from 1.35 months in the lowest quintile of feature_078 (30-56.5) to 6.24 months in the highest quintile (73.5-90). This single feature explains roughly 73% of the variance in pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_018)', data=df).fit()  # ANOVA vs. age-only model
analyses.result_summary: Race (feature_018) shows essentially identical mean pfs_months across all five groups (white 3.74, black 3.74, hispanic 3.74, asian 3.80, other 3.75). After adjusting for feature_078, the race contrast is non-significant (ANOVA p=0.77). No racial disparity in PFS is detectable.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_045)', data=df).fit()  # ANOVA vs. age-only model
analyses.result_summary: Insurance (feature_045) shows essentially identical mean pfs_months across the four groups (medicare 3.75, private 3.74, medicaid 3.74, uninsured 3.73). After adjusting for feature_078, ANOVA p=0.45. No insurance-status disparity in PFS is detectable.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_057 (3-level integer 0/1/2) is monotonically associated with pfs_months, with higher categories showing shorter pfs_months (consistent with a stage- or performance-status-like severity score).
feature_067 (5-level integer 6-10, plausibly Gleason score for prostate cancer) is negatively associated with pfs_months: higher Gleason -> shorter pfs_months.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_057')['pfs_months'].mean(); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Strong, ordered effect. Mean pfs_months is 4.68 (feature_057=0, n=17592), 3.49 (=1, n=24971), and 2.38 (=2, n=7437). Adjusted for feature_078, feature_057=1 reduces PFS by 1.18 months and feature_057=2 by 2.29 months versus level 0 (both p<1e-200). The effect is strictly monotonic and likely represents a severity/performance-status-like categorical.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_067)', data=df).fit()  # ANOVA vs. age-only
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months across feature_067 levels 6, 7, 8, 9, 10 is 3.71, 3.76, 3.74, 3.74, 3.70 -- essentially flat. Adjusted ANOVA p=0.038 driven by tiny negative coefficients of -0.02 to -0.023 for levels 8/9/10 vs. level 6. The hypothesis of a meaningful negative gradient is REFUTED; the magnitude is clinically negligible (<0.03 month).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 (binary, prevalence 55%) is associated with shorter pfs_months when the indicator is positive.

Among other binary features with prevalence 40-60% (potentially treatments or major comorbidities), several beyond feature_051 will have detectable associations with pfs_months after adjustment for feature_078.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_051', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_051=1 is associated with a 0.52-month REDUCTION in pfs_months versus feature_051=0 (univariate t-test p<1e-180; adjusted for feature_078 the effect is -0.518 months, p<1e-300). This is the largest binary-feature main effect in the dataset.

analyses.code: for f in [list of 16 binary features with prev 35-60%]: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + f', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Of 16 binary features with prevalence between 35% and 60%, only feature_051 (beta=-0.518, p<1e-300) and feature_027 (beta=+0.031, p=1e-3) reach significance after adjustment for feature_078. Most candidate 'treatment-like' binaries (feature_077, feature_011, feature_087, feature_108, feature_088, feature_112, feature_047, feature_037, feature_017, feature_095, feature_012, feature_089, feature_102, feature_074) have null main effects (|beta|<0.03 month, p>0.01). The hypothesis is partially supported: a single dominant binary effect plus one minor one.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 (right-skewed continuous, range 0.12-3622) is negatively associated with pfs_months (higher values -> shorter PFS), consistent with a tumor-burden marker.
feature_006 (continuous, range 0-24.6, mean 3.84) is negatively associated with pfs_months.

feature_009 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5, mean 3.80, plausibly serum albumin) is POSITIVELY associated with pfs_months (higher -> longer PFS, consistent with a protective nutritional/general-health marker).
feature_092 (continuous, range 48-830, mean 224) is negatively associated with pfs_months on the log scale.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_013', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_013 is significantly NEGATIVELY associated with pfs_months: beta=-0.0033 month per unit, p<1e-300, after adjusting for feature_078. Univariate Pearson r=-0.153.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_006', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_006 is strongly NEGATIVELY associated with pfs_months: beta=-0.077 month per unit, p<1e-300 (univariate r=-0.143). Each 1-unit increase costs about 0.08 months of PFS.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_009', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_009 is strongly POSITIVELY associated with pfs_months: beta=+0.484 month per unit (range 1.5-5.5, so end-to-end span ~1.9 months), p<1e-300. Effect is consistent across feature_078 tertiles (low: +0.46, mid: +0.49, high: +0.51). Direction matches the prior for an albumin/nutritional marker.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + np.log(feature_092)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: log(feature_092) is significantly negatively associated with pfs_months: beta=-0.10 month per log-unit (p=7.5e-14). Linear-scale effect is also significant (beta=-5.4e-4, p<1e-19) but small in magnitude.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for feature_078 (the dominant correlate), only a small subset of features (feature_057, feature_051, feature_006, feature_013, feature_009, feature_092) retain large, clinically meaningful effects on pfs_months; most of the 122 features will be null contributors.

analyses.code: for c in features: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + c', data=df).fit()  # rank by p-value
analyses.result_summary: Across all 122 features, after adjustment for feature_078, exactly 9 reach p<0.001: feature_057, feature_051, feature_006, feature_013, feature_009, feature_092, feature_109, feature_039, and feature_043 (with feature_027 at p=1e-3 marginal). All others (including all 5 race categories, 4 insurance categories, all Gleason levels, and the great majority of binary indicators) are null. Hypothesis SUPPORTED: a sparse signal in a high-dimensional feature space.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive feature_078 (age-like) effect on pfs_months is attenuated in patients with worse feature_057 categories: feature_078 x feature_057 interaction is negative.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 * C(feature_057)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Significant negative interaction. The feature_078 main slope is +0.180 month/unit in feature_057=0; the interaction terms are -0.0058 (level 1, p=8.6e-17) and -0.0217 (level 2, p=2e-112). In other words, the protective gradient of feature_078 is roughly 12% smaller at level 1 and 12% smaller again at level 2 (slope ~+0.158/unit at the worst level). Hypothesis SUPPORTED with a clinically meaningful magnitude.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_013 on pfs_months is concentrated in feature_051=0 patients and is attenuated when feature_051=1 (feature_051 x feature_013 interaction is positive).
The protective effect of feature_009 differs between feature_051=0 and feature_051=1 patients (feature_051 x feature_009 interaction is non-zero).
The negative feature_006 effect differs by feature_051 status (interaction non-zero).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_051 * feature_013', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Strong positive interaction. Main effect feature_013: beta=-0.018 (p<1e-300) in feature_051=0; interaction feature_051:feature_013 = +0.0156 (p<1e-280). In feature_051=1, the net feature_013 slope is essentially zero (-0.018 + 0.016 = -0.002). The harm of high feature_013 is therefore largely confined to the feature_051=0 subgroup. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_051 * feature_009', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction term feature_051:feature_009 = +0.0087, p=0.62 (NS). The +0.48-month/unit benefit of feature_009 is the same in feature_051=0 and feature_051=1 patients. Hypothesis REFUTED.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_051 * feature_006', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_051:feature_006 = +0.0002, p=0.93 (NS). The negative slope of feature_006 (-0.077 month/unit) is identical across feature_051 strata. Hypothesis REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_109 (binary, 10% prevalence) and feature_039 (binary, 10% prevalence) interact synergistically: the combination feature_109=1 AND feature_039=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than the sum of their individual effects (positive multiplicative interaction).
The negative feature_051 effect differs across feature_057 severity strata.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_109*feature_039', data=df).fit(); df.groupby(['feature_109','feature_039'])['pfs_months'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Striking positive interaction. Cell means: (109=0,039=0) 3.74; (109=0,039=1) 3.67; (109=1,039=0) 3.67; (109=1,039=1) 5.22. The main effects are individually NULL (beta=-0.016, p=0.34 for f109; beta=-0.024, p=0.14 for f039) but the interaction term is +1.43 months (p=4e-178). Only when BOTH indicators are positive does PFS jump by ~1.5 months. This is a classic synergistic biomarker pattern. Hypothesis STRONGLY SUPPORTED.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_051 * C(feature_057)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Main effect feature_051 = -0.544 in feature_057=0; interaction with feature_057=1 = +0.034 (p=0.009); with feature_057=2 = +0.049 (p=0.007). The feature_051 penalty is statistically slightly attenuated in worse feature_057 strata, but the magnitude (~10% attenuation) is small. SUPPORTED with modest effect size.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The feature_051 effect on pfs_months differs by feature_018 (race), with at least one race showing a meaningfully different magnitude.
The feature_057 severity effect on pfs_months differs by feature_018 (race), reflecting heterogeneous prognostic gradients across racial groups.
feature_045 (insurance) has a residual effect on pfs_months after adjusting for feature_018 (race) and feature_078 (age).
The race effect on pfs_months differs across insurance strata (feature_018 x feature_045 interaction non-zero).

analyses.code: for race in df.feature_018.unique(): ttest of pfs_months by feature_051 within stratum
analyses.result_summary: feature_051=1 minus feature_051=0 mean PFS difference is essentially identical across races: white -0.525, black -0.506, hispanic -0.529, asian -0.535, other -0.366 (n=977; lower precision). All five p-values are <0.005 with essentially overlapping effect sizes. Hypothesis REFUTED -- no meaningful racial heterogeneity in feature_051 impact.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) * C(feature_018)', data=df).fit() vs. additive model
analyses.result_summary: The feature_057 gradient is essentially identical across races. Within-race coefficients for feature_057=2 vs 0: white -2.29, black -2.27, hispanic -2.27, asian -2.32, other -2.30. ANOVA for the feature_057 x race interaction yields p=0.85 -- no detectable heterogeneity. Hypothesis REFUTED.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_018)', data=df).fit() vs. + C(feature_045)
analyses.result_summary: ANOVA for adding insurance to a model with feature_078 + race yields p=0.45. Insurance has no detectable residual effect. Hypothesis REFUTED.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_018) * C(feature_045)', data=df).fit()  # vs. additive
analyses.result_summary: The 4 x 5 race x insurance interaction term is non-significant (ANOVA p=0.35). PFS does not vary jointly by race and insurance after age adjustment. Hypothesis REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable model containing feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_006, feature_013, feature_009, feature_092, feature_109, feature_039, feature_043, feature_027, feature_018, feature_045, and feature_067 will explain the great majority (>=90%) of variance in pfs_months, confirming that the prognostic signal is dominated by feature_078 plus a sparse panel of biomarkers.
Within the worst-prognosis subgroup (feature_057=2), there is no detectable racial disparity in pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + feature_051 + feature_006 + feature_013 + feature_009 + feature_092 + feature_109 + feature_039 + feature_043 + feature_027 + C(feature_018) + C(feature_045) + C(feature_067)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Full multivariable OLS achieves R-squared = 0.9476. All eleven retained continuous/binary predictors are highly significant (|t|>4 for every term). Race, insurance, and Gleason categories contribute no meaningful incremental information (|coef|<=0.023, mostly NS). Hypothesis SUPPORTED: ~95% of pfs_months variance is captured by feature_078 plus a sparse set of about ten features.

analyses.code: df_sub = df[df.feature_057==2]; smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_018)', data=df_sub).fit() vs. age-only
analyses.result_summary: In the n=7437 patients with feature_057=2, race ANOVA p=0.96 after adjustment for feature_078. No racial disparity in PFS even within the worst-prognosis stratum. Hypothesis SUPPORTED (null finding).
